name: From Carmona to the Castle of the Dukes of Alba – circular via Alcolea del Río
name_original: Desde Carmona hasta el Castillo de los Duques de Alba – circular por Alcolea del Río
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Carmona offer varied landscapes across Andalusia, Spain. The region is characterized by its strategic location atop the Alcores ridge, providing panoramic views and featuring an escarpment with heights ranging from 100 to 220 meters. The terrain includes a mix of rugged rocks, plains, and rolling hills, with the fertile plains of La Vega dominated by cereal and olive groves. This agricultural landscape, shaped by millennia of farming, offers subtle variations in color throughout the seasons. Best road cycling routes around Carmona The most popular road cycling route is From Carmona to the Castle of the Dukes of Alba – circular via Alcolea del Río, a 34.8 miles (56.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 21 minutes to complete. This route features a moderate difficulty grade and explores the countryside surrounding Carmona. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Alcalá de Guadaíra – Old locomotive loop from Repsol, a moderate 50.8 miles (81.8 km) path. This route offers a longer ride through the agricultural plains and rolling hills of the region. Local road cyclists also love the Church of San Pedro, Carmona – Historic Center of Carmona loop from Carmona, a 33.0 miles (53.1 km) trail leading through the historic surroundings of Carmona, often completed in about 2 hours 11 minutes. Road cycling around Carmona is defined by the Alcores ridge, fertile agricultural plains, and rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, including 1 easy, 13 moderate, and 1 difficult route. The routes in Carmona are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 3000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Carmona's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Carmona?
A: Carmona's road cycling routes offer a diverse landscape. You'll encounter the strategic Alcores ridge, providing panoramic views, and an escarpment ranging from 100 to 220 meters in height. The terrain also features a mix of rugged rocks, rolling hills, and the fertile plains of La Vega, dominated by cereal and olive groves. This agricultural landscape provides subtle variations in color throughout the seasons.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of road cycling routes in Carmona?
A: The road cycling network around Carmona caters to various ability levels. Out of 15 routes, there is 1 easy route, 13 moderate routes, and 1 difficult route. This variety ensures options for both casual riders and those seeking more challenging climbs.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in Carmona?
A: While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed, the region offers varied terrain including the Vía Verde de los Alcores, which is known for its mostly sandy terrain and is suitable for different activities. For road cycling, the presence of an easy route suggests there are less demanding options that could be suitable for families, though parental discretion is advised regarding distance and elevation.

Q: What are some notable historical or natural attractions to see while cycling near Carmona?
A: Carmona is rich in attractions. You can explore historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4118677" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcázar of the Gate of Seville&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5393629" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta de Córdoba&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1134619" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carmona Old Town&lt;/a&gt; itself is a highlight. Natural features include the Alcores Escarpment and various viewpoints offering stunning vistas of the agricultural plains and the walled city. The Vía Verde de los Alcores also provides immersion in local nature.

Q: When is the best time of year to go road cycling in Carmona?
A: The region is particularly popular for cycling in February and October. These months likely offer pleasant temperatures and favorable conditions for exploring the varied landscapes of Carmona without the extreme heat of summer.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about their experience in Carmona?
A: The road cycling routes around Carmona are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 60 reviews. Over 3000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Carmona's varied terrain, indicating a positive and popular experience among riders.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available from Carmona?
A: Yes, several routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in Carmona. For example, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995972386/from-carmona-to-the-castle-of-the-dukes-of-alba-circular-via-alcolea-del-rio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Carmona to the Castle of the Dukes of Alba – circular via Alcolea del Río&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19361395" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Pedro, Carmona – Historic Center of Carmona loop from Carmona&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the historic surroundings.

Q: Can I find long-distance road cycling routes around Carmona?
A: Yes, there are options for longer rides. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40314371" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá de Guadaíra – Old locomotive loop from Repsol&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 50.8 miles (81.8 km) path, offering a substantial ride through the agricultural plains and rolling hills. Another long option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40011395" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Carmona – Carmona Old Town loop from Carmona&lt;/a&gt;, which is nearly 90 km.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots accessible by road bike?
A: Carmona's location atop the Alcores ridge naturally provides numerous viewpoints. These offer breathtaking panoramic vistas of the surrounding agricultural plains, the historic walled city, and the distinctive Alcores ridge, allowing cyclists to appreciate the interplay of urban and rural landscapes.

Q: Is public transport available to access cycling routes in Carmona?
A: Carmona is a well-connected town, and while specific public transport links directly to trailheads for cyclists are not detailed, the town itself is accessible. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or train connections to Carmona, and then plan your cycling route from there, considering the town's central location for many routes.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the agricultural plains of La Vega?
A: Many routes around Carmona traverse the fertile plains of La Vega. This extensive agricultural area is characterized by cereal and olive groves, offering a magnificent natural spectacle with subtle variations in color throughout the seasons. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40314371" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá de Guadaíra – Old locomotive loop from Repsol&lt;/a&gt; provide a longer ride through these picturesque agricultural landscapes.
tips: Carmona has several viewpoints, but the most well-known is the Mirador de la Asomada del Ribero, located at the entrance to the town, and also the Mirador del Parador, next to the Alcázar of King Pedro. Both offer spectacular views.
The ruins of the palace that belonged to the house of Alba. The castle burnt down in 1718, has not been touched since. You can enter.

waypoints.name: Ruins of the Castle of the Dukes of Alba
